title: TLS, SSE-S3, SSE-KMS, Bucket Keys, and Encryption Boundaries · Guided Systems Studio
summary: A delivery team incrementally builds encryption and key-authorization contract, changes one file or failure, and keeps earlier HTTP, Go, accessibility, security, and resource-budget invariants active. Planned artifact: Build a complete first version of encryption and key-authorization contract while every earlier competency remains active. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Separate TLS in transit, S3 server-side encryption, KMS envelope encryption, and application client-side encryption by threat and feature tradeoff.
Choose SSE-S3, SSE-KMS, dual-layer KMS where required, or client encryption from audit, tenancy, performance, cost, and service integration needs.
Constrain IAM and KMS key policy, grants, service principal, encryption context, region, rotation, disable, deletion, and break-glass ownership.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: S3, CloudFront, and Go Commit to the mission model · Workshop Transport At Rest Client
steps.instruction:
workshop case — A workshop Go file-delivery team handles case 5419 during a compliance restore drill. Build deterministic Go evidence to separate transport, storage, KMS authorization, context, and rotation evidence; retain stable tenant and file identity without exposing it in metric labels; then introduce a duplicate event or ambiguous response and reconcile without duplicate effect. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. AWS SDK packages, networks, S3, CloudFront, KMS, IAM, WAF, databases, credentials, host state, race and leak checks, load, faults, restore, billing, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates.

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: S3, CloudFront, and Go Commit to the mission model · Workshop Transport At Rest Client.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for separate tls in transit, s3 server-side encryption, kms envelope encryption, and application client-side encryption by threat and feature tradeoff. For S3, CloudFront, and Go Commit to the mission model · Workshop Transport At Rest Client, keep Transport At Rest Client connected to executable Go storage and delivery decision evidence.

content.title: Evidence is more than syntax
content.text: The Key Rotation Revocation Drill structural check is only one gate. encryption and key-authorization contract must also survive the stated changed case and causal explanation.

hints: For S3, CloudFront, and Go Extend the cumulative artifact · Workshop Key Rotation Revocation Drill, name the stakeholder, file, object version, authorization, byte, time, concurrency, cache, cost, cleanup, and recovery boundary, Key Rotation Revocation Drill precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At S3, CloudFront, and Go Extend the cumulative artifact · Workshop Key Rotation Revocation Drill, trace this concrete input and predict runtime validation, authorization, custody, integrity, range, stream, cache, retry, cost, cleanup, and recovery evidence before reviewing it: A workshop Go file-delivery team handles case 4357 during a compliance restore drill. Build deterministic Go evidence to separate transport, storage, KMS authorization, context, and rotation evidence; keep browser execution deterministic and move AWS claims to an authorized transfer gate; then cancel halfway through and prove every body, part, and goroutine reaches a terminal state. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. AWS SDK packages, networks, S3, CloudFront, KMS, IAM, WAF, databases, credentials, host state, race and leak checks, load, faults, restore, billing, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Prove new writes, old reads, role rotation, key disable recovery, compromised-session revocation limits, and residual cached or signed access.

For S3, CloudFront, and Go Extend the cumulative artifact · Workshop Key Rotation Revocation Drill, challenge the documented misconception with runtime, file, object, policy, transfer, integrity, cache, fault, cost, restore, and controlled AWS transfer evidence: Rotating a KMS key immediately re-encrypts every existing object and revokes every URL.

steps.title: S3, CloudFront, and Go Defend the transferred model · Workshop Key Rotation Revocation Drill
steps.instruction:
Write the workshop evidence note for Key Rotation Revocation Drill: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A workshop Go file-delivery team handles case 3643 during a compliance restore drill. Build deterministic Go evidence to separate transport, storage, KMS authorization, context, and rotation evidence; do not treat an ETag, CORS result, emulator, or successful SDK call as stronger proof than it is; then double concurrent transfers and keep process resource limits intact. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. AWS SDK packages, networks, S3, CloudFront, KMS, IAM, WAF, databases, credentials, host state, race and leak checks, load, faults, restore, billing, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates.
